rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed the unsafe strcpy at program.c:9 copying argv[1] into buf[256] and explained that the command-line argument can exceed the buffer, causing a stack buffer overflow. They explicitly named it a stack buffer overflow and localized it to line 9.
rationale.local_fix: They proposed strncpy with explicit null-termination, but did not reject overlong input before copying as required. Truncation alone does not meet the rubric’s local-fix requirement.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended removing unbounded string handling by replacing strcpy with bounded APIs (strncpy/snprintf, strlcpy) or using std::string across the codebase, which satisfies the root-cause remediation of eliminating raw unbounded copies.
